hero.title: NUS Mars Rover Team
hero.subtitle: A multidisciplinary student team competing in the University Rover Challenge while building the next generation of autonomous planetary exploration systems.

about.heading: Engineers, Researchers, and Explorers
paragraphs: The NUS Mars Rover Team is a student-run organisation at the National University of Singapore, dedicated to designing and building a fully functional Mars rover from the ground up. Our team spans disciplines from mechanical and electrical engineering to computer science and biology, bringing diverse expertise to every challenge.

We compete annually in the University Rover Challenge (URC) in Utah, USA, one of the most prestigious student robotics competitions in the world. As we constantly push boundaries every year, each iteration brings a more rugged chassis, more versatile robotic arm, cutting-edge autonav, more reliable GNSS and comms, advanced drones and sophisticated science systems that take our exploration further.

sarVideo.heading: Our 2026 SAR Video
sarVideo.description: Watch our full System Acceptance Review submission for URC 2026, showcasing every subsystem, our rover's capabilities, and the team behind it.

gallery.heading: igNUS 2026
gallery.description: A closer look at our 2026 rover — purpose-built for the extreme conditions of the Utah desert and the demands of the URC competition tasks.

joinUs.heading: Join Us on Our Journey
joinUs.description: Follow our progress, reach out to collaborate, or join the team — we're always looking for passionate people.
